name: SMBC
attribution: Saturday Morning Breakfast Cereal by Zach Weinersmith

comics.title: Spooky
comics.alt: Kelly told me this one was too weird, so please make it popular so she has to roll her eyes.

comics.title: Deal
comics.alt: Later the drug dealer gets nostalgic about how in the 90s economy you could give out the first one free.

comics.title: Bunker
comics.alt: We're going to build a recursively self-improving god-like machine intelligence that wants to destroy humanity, but it's OK because I have a hole.

comics.title: Duck
comics.alt: Idiot drawn to look like me because I am the idiot.

comics.title: Proof
comics.alt: The notebook has feedback from a nurse, cowboy, and plumber.

comics.title: Summary
comics.alt: I posted this as a joke about a script I shouldn't do, and bluesky economists pressured me into making it.

comics.title: Huh
comics.alt: Once I realized this, all those inept AI laundry-folding videos became hilarious.

comics.title: Surveil
comics.alt: I don't know if it's happened yet, but you can bet in the next few years someone will start fitting people out to collect sex training data.

comics.title: Gold
comics.alt: I contend that SMBC remains the comic with the funniest jokes about commodity pricing.

comics.title: Consciousness
comics.alt: From an idea in Aguera y Arcas' recent book What is Intelligence?

comics.title: Yes
comics.alt: This is one of those jokes where the moment I finish drawing it I realize someone has probably already done it. But then, given that this is recursive, there's at least some layer of Doom on Doom that has not yet been achieved.

comics.title: Proof
comics.alt: This sort of thing is why I will likely never write fantasy.

comics.title: Gland
comics.alt: If you disagree, find me a recent source specifically proving Descartes wrong.

comics.title: Speech
comics.alt: Just a reminder that I am available for commencement speeches, birthdays, and Bar Mitzvahs.

comics.title: Marshmallow
comics.alt: A note of appreciation to the 4 of you who started laughing on the first panel.

comics.title: Shorthand
comics.alt: SMBC will be at Davos this year, if someone at Davos looks at it on their phone.
